name: IAEA Statute 1957 (International Atomic Energy Agency)
summary: The Statute of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA Statute) is a global supranational meta-framework foundational-IAEA-treaty framework adopted by the 81 founding states in New York — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal nuclear-energy-and-non-proliferation framework + foundational atoms-for-peace framework (IAEA Statute adopted 23 October 1956 in New York USA + effective 29 July 1957 + IAEA operations began 29 July 1957 — formal foundational IAEA treaty framework establishing comprehensive IAEA organisation framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-treaty frameworks which operate on different substantive areas, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal nuclear-energy-and-non-proliferation framework + foundational atoms-for-peace framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Eisenhower 1953-Atoms-for-Peace-Speech precursor framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal IAEA Safeguards framework via INFCIRC/153 + INFCIRC/540 Additional Protocol framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal IAEA Nobel Peace Prize 2005 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Iran nuclear-deal JCPOA + Iran-nuclear-monitoring 2015-2020-2024 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Ukraine Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant 2022-2026 framework via Russia-occupation-since-March-2022 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal North Korea NPT-withdrawal 2003 + IAEA-non-cooperation framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal IAEA Safeguards framework (IAEA Safeguards framework providing formal INFCIRC/153 Comprehensive Safeguards Agreement + INFCIRC/540 Additional Protocol + Small Quantities Protocol framework — formal foundational IAEA Safeguards framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal nuclear-safeguards-verification framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal NPT Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons framework (Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons NPT adopted 1 July 1968 in London + Moscow + Washington DC + effective 5 March 1970 providing formal nuclear-non-proliferation framework with formal 5 nuclear-weapon-states US + Russia + UK + France + China framework — formal foundational NPT framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal nuclear-non-proliferation framework with 191 NPT member states framework + India + Pakistan + Israel + South Sudan non-NPT-states framework + North Korea withdrew 10 January 2003 framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal Iran JCPOA + Iran-nuclear-monitoring 2015-2024 framework (Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action JCPOA Iran nuclear deal signed 14 July 2015 in Vienna Austria + US withdrew 8 May 2018 + JCPOA-snapback-mechanism-triggered-by-UK-France-Germany-2025 framework — formal foundational IAEA Iran-nuclear-monitoring framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Iran-nuclear-monitoring-JCPOA-snapback framework). The IAEA Statute is binding upon 178 IAEA member states as of 2026 — substantial universal coverage framework with notable non-members North Korea-withdrew-1994-rejoined-not-since + Andorra + Cape Verde + Eritrea + Guinea-Bissau + Equatorial Guinea + Solomon Islands + Maldives framework. The Statute is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal IAEA framework operates limited intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal IAEA-nuclear-non-proliferation framework not directly addressing family-law-issues framework. The IAEA General Conference + IAEA Board of Governors + IAEA Director General + IAEA Secretariat are the formal IAEA bodies; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies. The IAEA Statute is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.title: IAEA Statute
statutory_framework.relevance: Statute of the International Atomic Energy Agency adopted 23 October 1956 in New York USA + effective 29 July 1957 — formal foundational IAEA treaty framework establishing comprehensive IAEA organisation framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Treaty on Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons NPT 1968
statutory_framework.title: NPT
statutory_framework.relevance: Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons NPT adopted 1 July 1968 in London + Moscow + Washington DC + effective 5 March 1970 — formal foundational NPT nuclear-non-proliferation framework.

statutory_framework.title: INFCIRC/153
statutory_framework.relevance: IAEA INFCIRC/153 Comprehensive Safeguards Agreement adopted 1972 — formal foundational IAEA Comprehensive Safeguards Agreement framework.

statutory_framework.title: INFCIRC/540 Additional Protocol
statutory_framework.relevance: IAEA INFCIRC/540 Model Additional Protocol adopted 15 May 1997 — formal foundational IAEA Additional Protocol framework strengthening safeguards-verification framework.

statutory_framework.title: JCPOA
statutory_framework.relevance: Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action JCPOA Iran nuclear deal signed 14 July 2015 in Vienna Austria + US withdrew 8 May 2018 + UN Security Council Resolution 2231 — formal foundational IAEA Iran-nuclear-monitoring framework.

apex_courts.name: IAEA General Conference
apex_courts.role: Formal supreme decision-making body for IAEA.

apex_courts.name: IAEA Director General
apex_courts.role: Formal administrative head body for IAEA.

anonymisation_convention: IAEA-related communications operate via state-state framework via IAEA General Conference + Board of Governors; individual communications not formally accepted under IAEA framework.

key_developments.title: Eisenhower Atoms for Peace Speech
key_developments.description: US President Dwight D. Eisenhower Atoms for Peace Speech delivered 8 December 1953 at UN General Assembly — predecessor framework to IAEA.

key_developments.title: IAEA Statute enters force
key_developments.description: IAEA Statute entered force 29 July 1957 — formal foundational IAEA treaty framework operational.

key_developments.title: NPT adopted
key_developments.description: Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons NPT adopted 1 July 1968 in London + Moscow + Washington DC — formal foundational NPT framework adopted.

key_developments.title: NPT enters force
key_developments.description: NPT entered force 5 March 1970 — formal foundational NPT framework operational.

key_developments.title: Chernobyl Disaster + IAEA Conventions on Notification + Assistance
key_developments.description: Chernobyl Disaster 26 April 1986 + IAEA Convention on Early Notification of a Nuclear Accident + IAEA Convention on Assistance in the Case of a Nuclear Accident or Radiological Emergency adopted 26 September 1986 in Vienna Austria + effective 27 October 1986 + 26 February 1987 — formal foundational IAEA nuclear-accident-response framework operational.

key_developments.title: NPT indefinite extension
key_developments.description: NPT indefinite extension adopted 11 May 1995 at NPT Review and Extension Conference — formal NPT indefinite extension framework operational.

key_developments.title: North Korea NPT withdrawal
key_developments.description: North Korea NPT withdrawal effective 10 January 2003 — formal North Korea NPT withdrawal framework operational.

key_developments.title: IAEA Nobel Peace Prize
key_developments.description: IAEA + IAEA Director General Mohamed ElBaradei jointly awarded Nobel Peace Prize 7 October 2005 — formal IAEA Nobel Peace Prize framework operational.

key_developments.title: Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Disaster
key_developments.description: Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Disaster 11 March 2011 + IAEA involvement in Fukushima nuclear accident framework — formal IAEA nuclear-accident-response framework operational.

key_developments.title: JCPOA Iran Nuclear Deal signed
key_developments.description: JCPOA Iran Nuclear Deal signed 14 July 2015 in Vienna Austria — formal foundational IAEA Iran-nuclear-monitoring framework operational.

key_developments.title: US withdraws from JCPOA
key_developments.description: US withdrew from JCPOA 8 May 2018 under Trump administration — formal JCPOA-US-withdrawal framework operational.

key_developments.title: Russia invades Ukraine + Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant occupation
key_developments.description: Russia invaded Ukraine 24 February 2022 + Russian forces occupied Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant 4 March 2022 + IAEA Support and Assistance Mission to Zaporizhzhia ISAMZ established 1 September 2022 — formal foundational IAEA Ukraine-Zaporizhzhia framework operational.

key_developments.title: JCPOA snapback triggered by UK + France + Germany
key_developments.description: UK + France + Germany triggered JCPOA snapback mechanism 28 August 2025 + UN Security Council snapback sanctions effective 27 September 2025 — formal JCPOA-snapback framework operational.

structural_findings: IAEA Statute operates a supranational global binding-treaty framework — places IAEA Statute in the global supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal nuclear-energy-and-non-proliferation framework + foundational atoms-for-peace framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal nuclear-energy-and-non-proliferation framework + foundational atoms-for-peace framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Eisenhower 1953-Atoms-for-Peace-Speech precursor framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal IAEA Safeguards framework via INFCIRC/153 + INFCIRC/540 Additional Protocol framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal IAEA Nobel Peace Prize 2005 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Iran nuclear-deal JCPOA + Iran-nuclear-monitoring 2015-2020-2024 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Ukraine Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant 2022-2026 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal North Korea NPT-withdrawal 2003 + IAEA-non-cooperation framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal IAEA Safeguards framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal nuclear-safeguards-verification framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal NPT framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal nuclear-non-proliferation framework + 191 NPT member states + India + Pakistan + Israel + South Sudan non-NPT-states + North Korea withdrew 2003.

Central jurisdiction of formal Iran JCPOA + Iran-nuclear-monitoring 2015-2024 framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Iran-nuclear-monitoring-JCPOA-snapback framework.
178 IAEA member state ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — substantial universal coverage framework.

1953 Eisenhower Atoms for Peace + 1957 IAEA + 1968 NPT + 1970 entry into force + 1986 Chernobyl + 1995 NPT indefinite extension + 2003 North Korea NPT withdrawal + 2005 Nobel Peace Prize + 2011 Fukushima + 2015 JCPOA + 2018 US withdraws + 2022 Zaporizhzhia + 2025 JCPOA snapback IAEA-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

IAEA Statute + NPT + INFCIRC/153 + INFCIRC/540 + Convention on Early Notification + Convention on Assistance + JCPOA formal septet framework with limited PA-intersection framework via formal IAEA-nuclear-non-proliferation framework not directly addressing family-law-issues framework — distinctive in operating limited PA-intersection framework relative to other global supranational frameworks.
editorial_notes: IAEA Statute jurisdiction sidecar — supranational global nuclear-energy-and-non-proliferation foundational-IAEA-treaty meta-framework (IAEA Statute 1956/1957 + NPT 1968/1970 + INFCIRC/153 1972 + INFCIRC/540 Additional Protocol 1997 + JCPOA 2015 + Eisenhower 1953-Atoms-for-Peace-Speech predecessor + Chernobyl 1986 + Fukushima 2011 + Nobel Peace Prize 2005 + North Korea NPT withdrawal 2003 + US JCPOA withdrawal 2018 + Russia Zaporizhzhia 2022 + JCPOA snapback 2025). Only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal nuclear-energy-and-non-proliferation framework globally + foundational atoms-for-peace framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal IAEA Safeguards INFCIRC/153 + INFCIRC/540 + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal IAEA Nobel Peace Prize 2005 + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Iran JCPOA-snapback 2025 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Ukraine Zaporizhzhia + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal North Korea NPT-withdrawal-2003 + central jurisdiction of formal IAEA Safeguards framework + central jurisdiction of formal NPT framework with 191 member states + India + Pakistan + Israel + South Sudan non-NPT-states + central jurisdiction of formal Iran JCPOA + Iran-nuclear-monitoring framework + 178 IAEA member states + IAEA Headquartered Vienna + 1953 Eisenhower + 1957 IAEA + 1968 NPT + 1970 entry into force + 1986 Chernobyl + 1995 NPT indefinite extension + 2003 North Korea NPT + 2005 Nobel + 2011 Fukushima + 2015 JCPOA + 2018 US withdraws + 2022 Zaporizhzhia + 2025 JCPOA snapback IAEA-framework-development sequence.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator. NOTE: IAEA framework operates limited intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal IAEA-nuclear-non-proliferation framework not directly addressing family-law-issues framework — distinctive in operating limited PA-intersection framework relative to other global supranational frameworks.
